item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Um. Most of those deaths - all but Dobrovolsky, Patsayev, and Volkov - were well below the Kármán line, during launch in the case of Challenger and reentry or landing for Soyuz 1 and Columbia. Perhaps it should be "All individuals listed during flights to above the Kármán line."?

I had a longer explanation but hit cancel on accident and really don't want to go through the details of spaceflight accidents again right now, it's always harsh stuff to read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
a thing that most, if not all, new readers won't get

and that's a fucking tragedy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This skip is not terrible, but not acceptable either. The effect has been seen before, the alien rosetta stone has been done before, the weird thing that keeps track of deaths has been done before. In the end, it's just a mishmash of repeated tropes done worse than each particular instance. The homage to a thing that most, if not all, new readers won't get is just the straw that broke the skipamel back. I'll downvote it.

item.title: Necro-post, I know
item.content:encoded: Looking at the original writer's profile, it seems that he moved to the RPC Authority site and took his work with him.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This article was missing the

« SCP-2721 | SCP-2722 | SCP-2723 »

I added it in.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Backstory

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If you barely care about this skip, is it worth keeping it posted? I apologize if I'm wrong, but this looks a lot more like an intra-site reference/politics/memorial or something than an SCP entry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why is this even here? Crashed spaceship or something that tells us we're all doomed and was probably carrying human passengers. No hook or backstory or anything.

item.title: To all those wondering what happened to the old SCP-2722:
item.content:encoded:
The article was unfortunately deleted by its author. This is a shame, because it was one of the best SCPs I have ever read.

I created a backup of the original version though, so we can all keep enjoying it. You can find the main article here, and the wonderful supplementary document here.

Please note that I am not the original author of this article. All credit goes to Von Pincier. I merely re-uploaded it into a sandbox, because I think this SCP is too good to be forgotten.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"A cargo manifest, written in Cantonese and amended in an unknown language, detailing intake of dextro-amino acid-based foodstuffs."

…Don't think I didn't see what you did there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not in the slightest. With Ihp's far-better article up, the eventual fate of this one is not a particular concern of mine. The circle is closed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is.. hmm. The problem with this weird, but very real. The problem with this is that it's not as good as Ihp's 2117. Nor is it as good as Video Killed the Radio Star, which may be a more closer point of comparison.

That might not be a problem under normal circumstances, but, well, I can't help but feel that we don't need two entirely separate- and, indeed, contradictory- articles to fill the hole the original 2722 left.

I hope that doesn't come across as an unfair reason for a downvote.

item.title: Re: Why the Stele?
item.content:encoded: They make for very good markers and memorials.

item.title: Why the Stele?
item.content:encoded:
But why is this a carbonated plant? I don't get it!
Why the stele?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Should this be added to the Stealing Solidarity hub?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why is Solidarity gone?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think you might need to footnote Karman Line, for the same reason that I reluctantly footnoted how shrikes hunt on SCP-2323.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The first two points are easily answered by Wikipedia. The third I will agree with. The fourth is predicated upon the article that held this slot previously. As for the fifth, it's the bodies of SCP-2085 - there's only one group on this site of five women and one dude who are associated with 2722, would name something "The Big Gun", and fly the banner of the black rabbit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Totally fair.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Don't get this at all, and it seems to make a lot of assumptions about knowledge the reader has.

  • Don't know what a stele is
  • Don't know what the karman line is
  • Mention of minimum safe distance comes out of nowhere is and pretty jarring.
  • Sudden mention that this is apparently a ship of some kind comes out of nowhere.
  • Don't understand the relevance of any of those recovered items.

Ultimately, I just don't get this at all. Feels either massively unfinished or the middle section of some story I've never read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, okay I guess. Novote on balance.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The point is mostly to plug the hole for my own personal satisfaction. A bit overly egotistical, perhaps, but the site climate being what it is and circumstances being what they are I decided that I'd indulge myself one final time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…I thought that symbol sounded familiar.

I then have to question whether this can stand on its own. I mean, Black Rabbit Company is a separate skip, and the tales about them taking the Solidarity persist as well.

But unless you've read those, and/or lived through the original 2722 before it was taken down, is this going to make any sense? Like, is the entire point of this homage, or am I still missing the picture?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The last two lines should give part of it away, at least.

One man, five women, who would name something "The Big Gun" and run around with a flag with a black rabbit on it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I do not get this. It's a creepy thing what keeps track of astronaut deaths and jabbers at you if you get too close. Also, it collects random space garbage?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
At least your thing will be on the site in some form, sooner or later. I guess it might work better as a tale.

But this? This would have been a fine skip in any other slot, but it feels like a poor replacement for the original SCP-2722.

RIP, SCPS Solidarity.

item.title: S
item.content:encoded: I really like your style of “Here’s some weird ass thing. Make of it what you will.”

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fix whatever you see that needs fixing. This was the coldest of cold posts.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Idk if these are intentional or not, but several "SCP-2722" instances in the text are improperly capitalized

I wanna fix but idk if this will mess up the article

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nevermind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Before anyone in the know gets on Djoric's case about this: I agreed to let him have this slot. The thing I'm working on is going to go up, still, whether as a tale or something else, I cannot say.

item.title: I thought I was out, they pull me back in
item.content:encoded: Unfinished business, now finished.
